texto: The enormous vermilion sun was dropping toward the sea, its reflected glow making a blazing path across the water to the very beach, where the last ripple was spangled with garnets. Otherwise, the sea was periwinkle purple, spilling and whispering and sidling with an easy going prattle of foam round the steeper rocks.
explicacao: Citação de L.M. Boston sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. “The enormous vermilion sun was dropping toward the sea, its reflected glow making a blazing path …” articula sabedoria, beach, calm em registro de sabedoria, com relevância para contextos como calm e ocean.

Otherwise, the sea was periwinkle purple, spilling and whispering and sidling with an easy going prattle of foam round the steeper rocks.

texto: She had short curls and her face had so many wrinkles it looked as if someone had been trying to draw her for a very long time and every line put in had made the face more like her.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a L.M. Boston: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “She had short curls and her face had so many wrinkles it looked as if someone had been trying to …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que tempo, age, description pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de description e faces.

texto: Then all was quiet, except for that murmurous half telling, half withholding of tremendous secrets that the sea would keep up all night. Each little wave seemed to say, “I’ll tell you-” and then pull back with a smothered “Oh!” to be followed by another wave saying, “Then I will say-” but whatever it was remained unsaid and unsayable.
explicacao: Citação de L.M. Boston sobre temas ligados a familia. “Then all was quiet, except for that murmurous half telling, half withholding of tremendous secret…” articula familia, ocean, sea em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como boa-noite e reflexao-noturna.

Each little wave seemed to say, “I’ll tell you-” and then pull back with a smothered “Oh!” to be followed by another wave saying, “Then I will say-” but whatever it was remained unsaid and unsayable.
